step_by_step_thinking:
I agree with the provided analysis. One additional point supporting why 'D' could be incorrect is that newer digital technologies have variable image quality depending on factors such as sensor sensitivity, software algorithms, and calibration. In contrast, some legacy analog systems were known for their consistency and reliability in producing clear images. Therefore, claiming that digital images are uniformly sharper than any possible analog system would be overly broad and inaccurate.
